excerpt: Minimum documented civilian casualties from 20 Jan to 7 Mar 2009 in the Mullaittivu conflict area.
confidence_note: Explicitly described as a minimum documented count.

excerpt: UN RC/HC minimum documented civilian deaths for the episode window.
transform_note: Used as the hard lower bound for the episode civilian direct-death estimate.
confidence_note: Conservative floor; document says minimum documented.

excerpt: UN RC/HC minimum documented civilian injuries for the episode window.
confidence_note: Conservative floor; document says minimum documented.

excerpt: Source gives a minimum documented count and says the true total is likely closer to 400.
transform_note: Best estimate left null at claim level because source gives minimum plus likely upper direction rather than a single point.
confidence_note: Strong subgroup evidence but still presented as a floor and likely-underestimate.

excerpt: Aid-agency estimate of civilians trapped in the battle zone on 8 Feb 2009.
transform_note: Denominator input, not a mortality claim.
confidence_note: Useful lower-start scenario; government estimate in same article was about half.

excerpt: AGA situation report stating about 81,000 families, totalling some 330,000 persons, were present in Mullaittivu District.
transform_note: Denominator input, not a mortality claim.
confidence_note: Important upper-start denominator anchor; later contested by the government.

geographic_scope: NFZ and adjacent conflict area, Mullaittivu District
excerpt: Satellite-based estimate of at least 100,000 people within the NFZ plus an estimated 30,000 outside the NFZ.
transform_note: Derived by summing the source's two explicit population components.

excerpt: In March 2009 the UN Resident Coordinator's Office indicated food needs for between 150,000 and 200,000 people.
transform_note: Midpoint 175,000 inserted from the explicit source range for denominator modeling. Month-level source statement anchored to 2009-03-01 for machine readability.
confidence_note: Useful as an end-of-window range, though broader than the 7 March UN RC snapshot.

excerpt: Contemporaneous Reuters report that more than 50,000 soldiers were converging on the pocket.
transform_note: Used as a floor for directly exposed Sri Lankan combatants.
confidence_note: Good floor; not a full order-of-battle.

excerpt: Contemporaneous Reuters report that the LTTE was estimated to number no more than 2,000 hardcore fighters.
transform_note: Used as LTTE component of combatant exposure floor.
confidence_note: Likely understates wider coerced or ad hoc fighting manpower, but useful as a hard core-fighter anchor.

geographic_scope: Sri Lanka conflict-wide, especially northern final phase
excerpt: LLRC records MoD submission that 4,264 LTTE deaths were confirmed by name for January-May 2009.
transform_note: Used as a named-victim floor for model-generating episode combatant deaths; not a clean episode total. Month boundaries expanded to first/last day for machine readability.
confidence_note: Official source and broad time window; should be treated as partial floor, not full E3 total.

excerpt: LLRC records security-force casualties for July 2006-May 2009 as 5,556 killed in action.
transform_note: Contextual ceiling/floor input showing government-side combatant losses were substantial but not episode-disaggregated. Month boundaries expanded to first/last day for machine readability.
confidence_note: Official source and conflict-wide period; not directly allocable to E3 without modeling.

excerpt: LLRC relays a U.S. State Department figure of 6,710 deaths from 20 Jan to 20 Apr 2009 while explicitly noting it did not differentiate between civilians and LTTE cadres.
transform_note: Kept outside the core direct-death path because the source itself says victim status is mixed.
confidence_note: Important warning against mechanical ingestion of later broad totals.

excerpt: HRW conservative tally of civilian deaths collected by independent observers during the first three weeks of the episode.
transform_note: Triangulation source for the lower half of the episode.
confidence_note: Conservative and shorter time window than the full episode.

excerpt: Feinstein recounts OHCHR public statement that more than 2,800 civilians may have been killed since 20 January.
transform_note: Lower-bound style triangulation claim only; source says more than 2,800.
confidence_note: Retrospective citation of OHCHR statement; supportive rather than primary.
